title: Publish the 1.1.0 GitHub Release (prep DONE - just tag-move + Gate 2 + publish)
description: All release prep is COMPLETE and verified as of 2026-07-22. Remaining = the public publish only, deferred to a later session by user. DONE: master at 7ffc3242 (UI fixes + notes + approval), release:check green, notes re-approved (.approved-1.1.0.json), 6 artifacts built + verified in D:/CubricStudio/Vision/Builds/v1.1.0/ (3 full + 3 delta, win/linux/macos-arm64), CI artifacts cleaned. Delta update 1.0.1 to 1.1.0 PROVEN live on a real install (18+ gate chains changelog clean, engine bumped 0.27.0, models preserved). TO PUBLISH (mpi-release steps 4-6): (1) MOVE the v1.1.0 tag to current master HEAD - it currently points at the pre-fix commit and MUST match shipped bytes (force-retag + force-push, or delete+recreate). NOTE: artifacts were built via workflow_dispatch on master so bytes already match HEAD - only the tag ref is stale. (2) Gate 2: user reviews the GitHub release body copy (github-release-checklist.md claim boundary + platform-disclosure block). (3) gh release create v1.1.0 --repo MadPonyInteractive/Cubric-Vision with all 6 artifacts (full builds + delta bundles - deltas are how existing users patch). Latest public release is currently v1.0.1; 1.1.0 has NO github release yet (Patreon-only zero-subs = effectively unreleased). Run the mpi-release skill for the full playbook.
